summary: Barber Hub v3.9.3 backend regression — 23/23 pytest passed. Validated (a) routers/vacation.py extracted endpoint behaves identically to server.py version (toggle on/off/with-messages), (b) new billing_cycle field on SubscriptionOrderCreate correctly picks yearly_price for cycle=yearly, monthly_price for cycle=monthly, rejects invalid cycles with 400 + proper detail, and falls back to monthly_price when yearly_price is null, (c) admin approve now produces activated_until ~365 days for yearly orders and ~30 days for monthly orders (plus free_trial_months*30 bonus), (d) listing filters (is_on_vacation + approval_status) now enforced on /api/barbers, /api/barbers/nearby, /api/ranking/top, /api/ranking/tiers — toggling vacation ON hides the test salon from all four, (e) gender segregation strict on /api/barbers, /api/barbers/nearby, /api/barbershops, /api/search/barbers, (f) admin GET /api/admin/subscription-orders/{id} includes admin_wa_link, (g) receipt validation (gif/invalid-base64/<100B) still 400s.

minor.issue: Accepts query param `shop_type` (not `type`) — inconsistent with /api/barbers + /api/barbers/nearby + /api/barbershops which accept `type`. If the SPA sends `type=female` here the server silently ignores it and returns mixed genders. Recommend accepting both `type` and `shop_type` on this endpoint (or rename for consistency).

Does NOT apply the approval_status filter that was added to the other five listing endpoints in v3.9.3. Only is_on_vacation is filtered. Non-approved / rejected salons can still appear here. Add the same $or filter used in /api/barbers (approval_status == approved OR approval_status missing + is_verified).

When an admin passes a non-None `duration_days`, the legacy yearly-safety-net (checking amount >= yearly_price*0.95) is skipped. This is fine for current tests but worth a comment. The legacy-order upgrade path only triggers when billing_cycle is missing AND duration_days isn't provided.

backend: TestBillingCycle::test_invalid_cycle_weekly_rejected (400 + detail contains 'billing_cycle must be monthly or yearly')
TestAdminApproveDurations::test_admin_detail_includes_wa_link (GET /api/admin/subscription-orders/{id} -> admin_wa_link starts with https://wa.me/ and carries ?text=)

action_items: (MINOR) /api/search/barbers accepts `shop_type` not `type` — inconsistent with the other four listing endpoints. Either alias or rename.

(MINOR) /api/search/barbers still lacks the approval_status filter that v3.9.3 added to /api/barbers, /api/barbers/nearby, /api/ranking/top, /api/ranking/tiers, /api/seo/city. Non-approved / rejected salons could surface in advanced search. Add the same $or filter.

(CLEANUP) Test-suite seeded several pending subscription orders with notes='TEST_v393' / 'TEST_v393_approve' on salon 0998765432 — consider a cron or fixture teardown that prunes TEST_-tagged orders periodically.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/vacation.py uses a build_router(db, require_barbershop) factory to avoid a server.py import cycle — clean pattern, recommend applying it to subscriptions/working_hours next. File is 50 LOC and focused.

admin_approve branch has a slightly subtle safety-net: `if not payload.duration_days and cycle == 'monthly' and amount >= yearly_price*0.95 -> upgrade to yearly`. Works, but the 0.95 tolerance is undocumented and could surprise ops. Consider adding a comment or a feature-flag.
server.py still 8177 LOC — only vacation was extracted in this iteration. The iteration_6 recommendation to extract subscriptions/working_hours still applies.

SubscriptionOrderCreate.billing_cycle defaults to 'monthly' but is NOT validated via Literal — defaults to str. Upgrading to `Literal['monthly','yearly']` in Pydantic would trigger 422 at the layer instead of 400 in-handler and give better OpenAPI docs.

Approval-status filter is consistently expressed as the same 3-line $or block in 6 places — extract to a module-level helper (e.g. _active_shop_query(shop_type=None) -> Dict) to keep them in sync forever.
seed_data_creation: Added ~6 TEST_-tagged pending/approved subscription_orders to db.subscription_orders for salon 0998765432 (notes starting with TEST_v393). Test salon's subscription_expiry was extended (approve tests run every iteration). Vacation toggled on/off several times — final state: is_on_vacation=false.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_v393_backend.py as the v3.9.3 regression suite (23 tests, all green). Run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL exported. The admin_approve tests append ~425 days (yearly) + ~90 days (monthly) to the salon's subscription_expiry every run — consider resetting periodically. Two gaps remain on /api/search/barbers: query param name (`shop_type` vs `type`) and missing approval_status filter — flagged to main agent as MINOR. Gender segregation only has ONE male salon in the DB to test against; to exercise cross-pollution a female salon would need to be seeded.
rca of the issue: No critical issues. Two minor endpoint-consistency gaps on /api/search/barbers: (a) param name is `shop_type` instead of `type` — SPA may silently be ignored; (b) approval_status filter was not added in this iteration's pass. Reproduce: curl 'BASE/api/search/barbers?type=female' -> returns male salons because param `type` is dropped by FastAPI. Mitigation: add `type: Optional[str] = None` alias and add the same approval $or filter used on the 5 fixed endpoints.
